content.thinking:
The user is adding a new configuration variable EXCLUDE_LOCK_COLUMNS_FROM_STATIC to control whether col 0/5 should be excluded from the static image rebuilding process in the RespinLockedSet.forEach loop. This is a feature addition that allows toggling between:
- true (default): skip col 0/5 when rebuilding static images (since they're already locked via SetColumnLock)
- false: rebuild static images for all entries including col 0/5 (old behavior)

The comment explains the rationale: col 0/5 are locked by SetColumnLock (reel doesn't spin, original symbols remain), so there's no need to overlay additional static images via EffectPlate. This is in preparation for server changes where RespinLockedPos might include col 0/5 entries.

  <narrative>Added a new configuration constant EXCLUDE_LOCK_COLUMNS_FROM_STATIC to control static image rebuilding behavior for locked columns 0 and 5. The flag is set to true by default, which will skip these columns during the RespinLockedSet.forEach loop that rebuilds static images via EffectPlate. The rationale documented in the comment explains that col 0/5 are already locked by SetColumnLock (preventing reel rotation, so original symbols naturally persist), making additional static image overlays unnecessary. This change anticipates a server protocol modification where RespinLockedPos will include col 0/5 entries in the cumulative set. Setting the flag to false allows reverting to the old behavior of rebuilding static images for all locked entries. The physical locking via SetColumnLock remains unchanged regardless of this flag's value.</narrative>

content.thinking:
This is the key implementation! The user wanted to add a variable to control whether to exclude col 0/5 from the RespinLockedSet processing. Now the actual filtering logic has been added to the forEach loop.

The implementation adds:
1. A check at the beginning of the forEach callback
2. If EXCLUDE_LOCK_COLUMNS_FROM_STATIC is true AND the col is in RespinLockColumns (0 or 5), it returns early
3. This skips all the static image rebuilding for those columns

This is the core feature implementation - it filters out col 0/5 from the visual processing while keeping the physical locking intact.

<observation>
  <type>feature</type>
  <title>Implemented conditional exclusion of col 0/5 from static image rebuilding</title>
  <subtitle>RespinLockedSet forEach loop now skips col 0/5 when EXCLUDE_LOCK_COLUMNS_FROM_STATIC is true</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>Added early return in RespinLockedSet.forEach when EXCLUDE_LOCK_COLUMNS_FROM_STATIC is true and col is in RespinLockColumns</fact>
    <fact>Filter uses Game_Define.RespinLockColumns.includes(col) to check if column is 0 or 5</fact>
    <fact>Skipped operations include CleanSymbolSpineAt, HideSymbolByRow, and CreateSymbolStaticNode for col 0/5</fact>
    <fact>Physical column locking via SetColumnLock remains unchanged regardless of exclusion flag</fact>
    <fact>Col 0/5 symbols naturally persist because reels don't spin when locked, eliminating need for EffectPlate overlays</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>Implemented the core filtering logic to conditionally exclude columns 0 and 5 from static image rebuilding in HandleReel's RespinLockedSet.forEach loop. When EXCLUDE_LOCK_COLUMNS_FROM_STATIC is enabled (default true), the code checks if the current entry's column is in Game_Define.RespinLockColumns using includes(col), and returns early if true. This skips the visual processing operations: cleaning residual Spine nodes, hiding original symbols, and creating EffectPlate static overlays. The rationale is that col 0/5 are already physically locked by SetColumnLock, preventing reel rotation, so original symbols naturally remain visible without requiring additional static image overlays. This implementation addresses the user's requirement to handle the new server protocol where RespinLockedPos includes col 0/5 entries, while avoiding redundant visual processing for columns that are already visually stable due to physical locking.</narrative>
